html:
𐤉𐤄𐤅𐤄

THE ANCIENT COVENANT RECORD

Volume Three — Part One

The Torah — Third Book

VAYIKRA

And He Called

Chapters 1 through 16

The Offerings — The Priesthood — Purity Laws — Yom Kippur

PRIMARY MANUSCRIPT AUTHORITIES

4QLeva (4Q23) · 4QLevb (4Q24) · 4QLevc (4Q25) · 4QLevd (4Q26)

4QLeve (4Q26a) · 4QLevf (4Q26b) · 11QLeva (11Q1) — Cave 11 Leviticus scroll

4QpaleoLev-Num (4Q23) — Paleo-Hebrew script

Ethiopian Orit Ge'ez — Beta Israel Ancient Canon

COMPARATIVE NOTES AT END OF EVERY CHAPTER

DSS · ORIT GE'EZ · MASORETIC VARIANT

YHWH / 𐤉𐤄𐤅𐤄 and The Creator only — no substitutions

All personal names in original Hebrew form throughout

THE OFFERINGS — CHAPTERS 1 THROUGH 7

VAYIKRA — CHAPTER 1 The Burnt Offering — Olah — From the Herd, Flock, and Birds

1:1 YHWH called to Moshe and spoke to him from the tent of meeting, saying:

1:2 Speak to the people of Yisra'EL and say to them: When any one of you brings an offering to YHWH, you shall bring your offering of livestock from the herd or from the flock.

1:3 If his offering is a burnt offering from the herd, he shall offer a male without blemish. He shall bring it to the entrance of the tent of meeting, that he may be accepted before YHWH.

1:4 He shall lay his hand on the head of the burnt offering, and it shall be accepted for him to make atonement for him.

1:5 Then he shall kill the bull before YHWH, and Aharon's sons the priests shall bring the blood and throw the blood against the sides of the altar that is at the entrance of the tent of meeting.

1:8 And Aharon's sons the priests shall arrange the pieces, the head, and the fat, on the wood that is on the fire on the altar.

1:9 But its entrails and its legs he shall wash with water. And the priest shall burn all of it on the altar, as a burnt offering, a food offering with a pleasing aroma to YHWH.

1:10 If his gift for a burnt offering is from the flock, from the sheep or goats, he shall bring a male without blemish.

1:14 If his offering to YHWH is a burnt offering of birds, then he shall bring his offering of turtledoves or pigeons.

1:17 And he shall tear it open by its wings, but shall not sever it completely. And the priest shall burn it on the altar, on the wood that is on the fire. It is a burnt offering, a food offering with a pleasing aroma to YHWH.

COMPARATIVE MANUSCRIPT NOTES

[DSS] 4QLeva and 11QLeva preserve portions of Chapter 1. The burnt offering legislation is attested in multiple Cave 4 and Cave 11 manuscripts. 11QLeva is one of the best-preserved Leviticus scrolls from Qumran and covers large sections of Chapters 1 through 27.

[ORIT GE'EZ] The Orit Ge'ez preserves Chapter 1 in full.

[MASORETIC VARIANT] No significant variants from the DSS in this chapter.

[CRITICAL NOTE] Verse 1:4 — he shall lay his hand on the head of the burnt offering, and it shall be accepted for him to make atonement. The laying of the hand — semikhah — transfers the identity of the offerer to the animal. The animal dies in the place of the one bringing it. Atonement — kapparah — is the covering of the breach between the offerer and YHWH. The entire sacrificial system of Vayikra is built on this principle of substitution and covenant restoration. The burnt offering — olah, that which goes up — is fully consumed. Nothing is retained by the offerer. It is total giving.

VAYIKRA — CHAPTER 2 The Grain Offering — Minkhah

2:1 When anyone brings a grain offering as an offering to YHWH, his offering shall be of fine flour. He shall pour oil on it and put frankincense on it

2:2 And bring it to Aharon's sons the priests. And he shall take from it a handful of the fine flour and oil, with all of its frankincense, and the priest shall burn this as its memorial portion on the altar, a food offering with a pleasing aroma to YHWH.

2:3 But the rest of the grain offering shall be for Aharon and his sons — it is a most holy part of YHWH's food offerings.

2:11 No grain offering that you bring to YHWH shall be made with leaven, for you shall burn no leaven nor any honey as a food offering to YHWH.

2:12 As an offering of firstfruits you may bring them to YHWH, but they shall not be offered on the altar for a pleasing aroma.

2:13 You shall season all your grain offerings with salt. You shall not let the salt of the covenant with your Creator be missing from your grain offering — with all your offerings you shall offer salt.

COMPARATIVE MANUSCRIPT NOTES

[DSS] 4QLeva and 11QLeva preserve portions of Chapter 2.

[ORIT GE'EZ] The Orit Ge'ez preserves Chapter 2 in full.

[MASORETIC VARIANT] No significant variants from the DSS in this chapter.

[CRITICAL NOTE] Verse 2:13 — you shall not let the salt of the covenant with your Creator be missing from your grain offering. Salt is the covenant element present in every grain offering. Salt preserves — it does not decay. The covenant of salt is an everlasting covenant — referenced in Bamidbar 18:19 and Divrei HaYamim Bet 13:5. Every offering brought to YHWH must carry the mark of the everlasting covenant. Salt connects the temporary act of offering to the permanent nature of the covenant relationship.

VAYIKRA — CHAPTER 3 The Peace Offering — Shelamim

3:1 If his offering is a sacrifice of peace offering, if he offers an animal from the herd, male or female, he shall offer it without blemish before YHWH.

3:2 And he shall lay his hand on the head of his offering and kill it at the entrance of the tent of meeting, and Aharon's sons the priests shall throw the blood against the sides of the altar.

3:3 And from the sacrifice of the peace offering, as a food offering to YHWH, he shall offer the fat covering the entrails and all the fat that is on the entrails,

3:5 Then Aharon's sons shall burn it on the altar on top of the burnt offering, which is on the wood on the fire — it is a food offering with a pleasing aroma to YHWH.

3:16 And the priest shall burn them on the altar as a food offering with a pleasing aroma. All fat is YHWH's.

3:17 It shall be a statute forever throughout your generations, in all your dwelling places, that you eat neither fat nor blood.

COMPARATIVE MANUSCRIPT NOTES

[DSS] 4QLeva and 11QLeva preserve portions of Chapter 3.

[ORIT GE'EZ] The Orit Ge'ez preserves Chapter 3 in full.

[MASORETIC VARIANT] No significant variants from the DSS in this chapter.

[CRITICAL NOTE] Verse 3:17 — you eat neither fat nor blood. The prohibition of fat and blood runs throughout Vayikra. Blood is the life of the creature — Vayikra 17:11 — and belongs to YHWH for atonement. Fat is the richest portion, YHWH's portion. These two prohibitions are among the most consistently maintained in the ancient Hebrew dietary law across all textual traditions — DSS, Orit, and the archaeological record of ancient Israelite practice.

VAYIKRA — CHAPTER 4 The Sin Offering — Hattat — For the Priest, the Congregation, the Leader, and the Individual

4:1 And YHWH spoke to Moshe, saying:

4:2 Speak to the people of Yisra'EL, saying: If anyone sins unintentionally in any of YHWH's commandments about things not to be done, and does any one of them —

4:3 If it is the anointed priest who sins, thus bringing guilt on the people, then he shall offer for the sin that he has committed a bull from the herd without blemish to YHWH for a sin offering.

4:4 He shall bring the bull to the entrance of the tent of meeting before YHWH and lay his hand on the head of the bull and kill the bull before YHWH.

4:13 If the whole congregation of Yisra'EL sins unintentionally and the thing is hidden from the eyes of the assembly, and they do any one of the things that by YHWH's commandments ought not to be done, and they realize their guilt —

4:14 When the sin which they have committed becomes known, the assembly shall offer a bull from the herd for a sin offering and bring it in front of the tent of meeting.

4:20 Thus shall he do with the bull. As he did with the bull of the sin offering, so shall he do with this. And the priest shall make atonement for them, and they shall be forgiven.

4:22 When a leader sins, doing unintentionally any one of all the things that by the commandments of YHWH his Creator ought not to be done, and realizes his guilt,

4:27 If anyone of the common people sins unintentionally in doing any one of the things that by YHWH's commandments ought not to be done, and realizes his guilt,

4:31 And all its fat he shall remove, as the fat is removed from the peace offering, and the priest shall burn it on the altar for a pleasing aroma to YHWH. And the priest shall make atonement for him, and he shall be forgiven.

COMPARATIVE MANUSCRIPT NOTES

[DSS] 4QLeva and 11QLeva preserve portions of Chapter 4.

[ORIT GE'EZ] The Orit Ge'ez preserves Chapter 4 in full.

[MASORETIC VARIANT] No significant variants from the DSS in this chapter.

[CRITICAL NOTE] The sin offering legislation distinguishes four categories of offerer: the anointed priest, the whole congregation, a leader, and an individual from the common people. The offering required and the procedure differ by category. The ancient Hebrew legal system embedded in Vayikra recognizes that moral accountability scales with position. The anointed priest who sins brings guilt on the people — verse 4:3. Leadership multiplies the consequence of failure. The covenant law does not treat all transgressions identically — it calibrates responsibility to role.

VAYIKRA — CHAPTER 5 The Guilt Offering — Asham — Specific Violations

5:1 If anyone sins in that he hears a public adjuration to testify, and though he is a witness, whether he has seen or come to know the matter, yet does not speak, he shall bear his iniquity.

5:4 Or if anyone utters with his lips a rash oath to do evil or to do good, any sort of rash oath that people swear, and it is hidden from him, when he comes to know it, and he realizes his guilt in any of these —

5:5 When he realizes his guilt in any of these and confesses the sin he has committed,

5:6 He shall bring to YHWH as his compensation for the sin that he has committed, a female from the flock, a lamb or a goat, for a sin offering. And the priest shall make atonement for him for his sin.

5:11 But if he cannot afford two turtledoves or two pigeons, then he shall bring as his offering for the sin that he has committed a tenth of an ephah of fine flour for a sin offering.

5:13 And the priest shall make atonement for him for the sin that he has committed in any one of these things, and he shall be forgiven. And the remainder shall be for the priest, as in the grain offering.

5:15 If anyone commits a breach of faith and sins unintentionally in any of the holy things of YHWH, he shall bring to YHWH as his compensation, a ram without blemish out of the flock, valued in silver shekels, according to the shekel of the sanctuary, for a guilt offering.

5:17 If anyone sins, doing any of the things that by YHWH's commandments ought not to be done, though he did not know it, then realizes his guilt, he shall bear his iniquity.

COMPARATIVE MANUSCRIPT NOTES

[DSS] 4QLeva and 11QLeva preserve portions of Chapter 5.

[ORIT GE'EZ] The Orit Ge'ez preserves Chapter 5 in full.

[MASORETIC VARIANT] No significant variants from the DSS in this chapter.

[CRITICAL NOTE] Verse 5:11 — if he cannot afford two turtledoves or two pigeons, then he shall bring a tenth of an ephah of fine flour. The sacrificial system of Vayikra is economically tiered. The wealthy bring a bull. Those with moderate means bring a goat or sheep. The poor bring two birds. The very poor bring grain. No one is excluded from atonement by poverty. The covenant law accommodates every economic level. YHWH does not price the poor out of forgiveness.

VAYIKRA — CHAPTER 6 The Offerings Continued — Instructions for the Priests

6:2 Command Aharon and his sons, saying: This is the law of the burnt offering. The burnt offering shall be on the hearth on the altar all night until the morning, and the fire of the altar shall be kept burning on it.

6:5 The fire on the altar shall be kept burning on it. It shall not go out. The priest shall burn wood on it every morning, and he shall arrange the burnt offering on it and shall burn on it the fat of the peace offerings.

6:6 Fire shall be kept burning on the altar continually — it shall not go out.

6:8 And YHWH spoke to Moshe, saying:

6:9 Speak to Aharon and his sons, saying: This is the law of the grain offering. The sons of Aharon shall offer it before YHWH, in front of the altar.

6:12 And the priest shall put on his linen garment and put his linen undergarment on his body, and he shall take up the ashes to which the fire has reduced the burnt offering on the altar and put them beside the altar.

6:13 Fire shall be kept burning on the altar continually — it shall not go out.

6:19 YHWH spoke to Moshe, saying:

6:20 This is the offering that Aharon and his sons shall offer to YHWH on the day when he is anointed: a tenth of an ephah of fine flour as a regular grain offering, half of it in the morning and half in the evening.

6:22 The priest from among his sons who is anointed to succeed him shall offer it. It is a statute forever to YHWH — it shall be made into smoke in its entirety.

COMPARATIVE MANUSCRIPT NOTES

[DSS] 4QLeva and 11QLeva preserve portions of Chapter 6.

[ORIT GE'EZ] The Orit Ge'ez preserves Chapter 6 in full.

[MASORETIC VARIANT] No significant variants from the DSS in this chapter.

[CRITICAL NOTE] Verse 6:13 — fire shall be kept burning on the altar continually — it shall not go out. The perpetual fire on the altar is one of the covenant's continuous obligations. It is not lit for each offering — it is never extinguished. The fire of YHWH's altar burns without ceasing, maintained by the daily labor of the priests, as a permanent sign of continuous covenant relationship. The moment the fire goes out is a covenant breach. Continuous fire means continuous access to YHWH's presence through the sacrificial system.

VAYIKRA — CHAPTER 7 The Guilt Offering Law — The Peace Offering Law — Fat and Blood Prohibited

7:1 This is the law of the guilt offering. It is most holy.

7:2 In the place where they kill the burnt offering they shall kill the guilt offering, and its blood shall be thrown against the sides of the altar.

7:11 And this is the law of the sacrifice of peace offerings that one may offer to YHWH.

7:12 If he offers it for a thanksgiving, then he shall offer with the thanksgiving offering unleavened loaves mixed with oil, unleavened wafers smeared with oil, and loaves of fine flour well mixed with oil.

7:15 And the flesh of the sacrifice of his peace offering for thanksgiving shall be eaten on the day of his offering. He shall not leave any of it until the morning.

7:22 And YHWH spoke to Moshe, saying:

7:23 Speak to the people of Yisra'EL, saying: You shall eat no fat, of ox or sheep or goat.

7:26 Moreover, you shall eat no blood whatever, whether of fowl or of animal, in any of your dwelling places.

7:27 Whoever eats any blood, that person shall be cut off from his people.

7:37 This is the law of the burnt offering, of the grain offering, of the sin offering, of the guilt offering, of the ordination offering, and of the peace offering,

7:38 Which YHWH commanded Moshe on Mount Sinai, on the day that he commanded the people of Yisra'EL to bring their offerings to YHWH, in the wilderness of Sinai.

COMPARATIVE MANUSCRIPT NOTES

[DSS] 4QLeva and 11QLeva preserve portions of Chapter 7.

[ORIT GE'EZ] The Orit Ge'ez preserves Chapter 7 in full.

[MASORETIC VARIANT] No significant variants from the DSS in this chapter.

[CRITICAL NOTE] Verse 7:37-38 — the summary statement closing the seven-chapter offering legislation traces the entire system back to Mount Sinai and YHWH's direct command to Moshe. The offering system is not a development of Israelite religious practice over time. It is declared as covenant law given at Sinai in the wilderness. The ancient record is explicit about the origin: YHWH commanded Moshe. This is the authoritative ground of the entire Vayikra legislation.

THE PRIESTHOOD — THE NADAV AND AVIHU ACCOUNT — CHAPTERS 8 THROUGH 10

VAYIKRA — CHAPTER 8 The Ordination of Aharon and His Sons

8:1 YHWH spoke to Moshe, saying:

8:2 Take Aharon and his sons with him, and the garments and the anointing oil and the bull of the sin offering and the two rams and the basket of unleavened bread.

8:3 And assemble all the congregation at the entrance of the tent of meeting.

8:6 And Moshe brought Aharon and his sons and washed them with water.

8:7 And he put the coat on him and tied the sash around his waist and clothed him with the robe and put the ephod on him and tied the skillfully woven band of the ephod around him, binding it to him.

8:8 And he placed the breastpiece on him, and in the breastpiece he put the Urim and the Thummim.

8:10 Then Moshe took the anointing oil and anointed the Mishkan and all that was in it, and consecrated them.

8:12 And he poured some of the anointing oil on Aharon's head and anointed him to consecrate him.

8:22 Then he presented the other ram, the ram of ordination, and Aharon and his sons laid their hands on the head of the ram.

8:23 And he killed it, and Moshe took some of its blood and put it on the lobe of Aharon's right ear and on the thumb of his right hand and on the big toe of his right foot.

8:30 Then Moshe took some of the anointing oil and of the blood that was on the altar and sprinkled it on Aharon and his garments, and also on his sons and his sons' garments. So he consecrated Aharon and his garments, and his sons and his sons' garments with him.

8:33 And you shall not go outside the entrance of the tent of meeting for seven days, until the days of your ordination are completed, for it will take seven days to ordain you.

8:35 You shall remain at the entrance of the tent of meeting day and night for seven days, performing what YHWH has charged, so that you do not die, for so I have been commanded.

8:36 And Aharon and his sons did all the things that YHWH commanded by Moshe.

COMPARATIVE MANUSCRIPT NOTES

[DSS] 4QLeva and 11QLeva preserve portions of Chapter 8.

[ORIT GE'EZ] The Orit Ge'ez preserves Chapter 8 in full.

[MASORETIC VARIANT] No significant variants from the DSS in this chapter.

[CRITICAL NOTE] Verse 8:8 — he placed the breastpiece on him, and in the breastpiece he put the Urim and the Thummim. The Urim and Thummim — the instruments of divine inquiry worn on the high priest's breastpiece — are placed there at ordination. Their exact nature is not described in the Torah text. The ancient record simply states they are placed in the breastpiece and worn by the high priest. Their function — determining YHWH's will in specific covenant matters — is referenced throughout the Nevi'im. They are the physical instrument of prophetic consultation for the high priest.

VAYIKRA — CHAPTER 9 The Eighth Day — Aharon's First Offerings — The Glory Appears

9:1 On the eighth day Moshe called Aharon and his sons and the elders of Yisra'EL,

9:2 And he said to Aharon: Take for yourself a bull calf for a sin offering and a ram for a burnt offering, both without blemish, and offer them before YHWH.

9:3 And say to the people of Yisra'EL: Take a male goat for a sin offering, and a calf and a lamb, both a year old without blemish, for a burnt offering,

9:4 And an ox and a ram for peace offerings, to sacrifice before YHWH, and a grain offering mixed with oil, for today YHWH will appear to you.

9:6 And Moshe said: This is the thing that YHWH commanded you to do, that the glory of YHWH may appear to you.

9:22 Then Aharon lifted up his hands toward the people and blessed them, and he came down from offering the sin offering and the burnt offering and the peace offerings.

9:23 And Moshe and Aharon went into the tent of meeting, and when they came out they blessed the people, and the glory of YHWH appeared to all the people.

9:24 And fire came out from before YHWH and consumed the burnt offering and the pieces of fat on the altar, and when all the people saw it, they shouted and fell on their faces.

COMPARATIVE MANUSCRIPT NOTES

[DSS] 4QLeva preserves portions of Chapter 9.

[ORIT GE'EZ] The Orit Ge'ez preserves Chapter 9 in full.

[MASORETIC VARIANT] No significant variants from the DSS in this chapter.

[CRITICAL NOTE] Verse 9:24 — fire came out from before YHWH and consumed the burnt offering. The fire that consumes Aharon's first offering is not kindled by human hands. It comes out from before YHWH. This divine fire is the same fire described in the Chanokh literature as the fire before the throne of YHWH. It consecrates the priesthood and the altar simultaneously. The people's response — they shouted and fell on their faces — is the appropriate response to a theophany. The sacrificial system is inaugurated by YHWH's direct action, not human ceremony alone.

VAYIKRA — CHAPTER 10 Nadav and Avihu — Strange Fire — Laws for the Priests

10:1 Now Nadav and Avihu, the sons of Aharon, each took his censer and put fire in it and laid incense on it and offered unauthorized fire before YHWH, which he had not commanded them.

10:2 And fire came out from before YHWH and consumed them, and they died before YHWH.

10:3 Then Moshe said to Aharon: This is what YHWH has said: Among those who are near me I will be sanctified, and before all the people I will be glorified. And Aharon held his peace.

10:4 And Moshe called Mishael and Eltzafan, the sons of Uzzi'el the uncle of Aharon, and said to them: Come near — carry your brothers away from the front of the sanctuary and out of the camp.

10:6 And Moshe said to Aharon and to El'azar and Itamar his sons: Do not let the hair of your heads hang loose, and do not tear your clothes, lest you die, and wrath come upon all the congregation. But let your brothers, the whole house of Yisra'EL, bewail the burning that YHWH has kindled.

10:8 And YHWH spoke to Aharon, saying:

10:9 Drink no wine or strong drink, you or your sons with you, when you go into the tent of meeting, lest you die. It shall be a statute forever throughout your generations.

10:10 You are to distinguish between the holy and the common, and between the unclean and the clean,

10:11 And you are to teach the people of Yisra'EL all the statutes that YHWH has spoken to them by Moshe.

10:16 Now Moshe diligently inquired about the goat of the sin offering, and behold, it had been burned up — and he was angry with El'azar and Itamar, the surviving sons of Aharon, saying:

10:17 Why have you not eaten the sin offering in the place of the sanctuary, since it is a thing most holy and has been given to you that you may bear the iniquity of the congregation, to make atonement for them before YHWH?

10:19 And Aharon said to Moshe: Behold, today they have offered their sin offering and their burnt offering before YHWH, and yet such things as these have happened to me. If I had eaten the sin offering today, would it have been acceptable in the sight of YHWH?

10:20 And when Moshe heard that, he approved.

COMPARATIVE MANUSCRIPT NOTES

[DSS] 4QLeva preserves portions of Chapter 10. The account of Nadav and Avihu is attested in the Cave 4 manuscripts.

[ORIT GE'EZ] The Orit Ge'ez preserves Chapter 10 in full.

[MASORETIC VARIANT] No significant variants from the DSS in this chapter.

[CRITICAL NOTE] Verse 10:1-2 — unauthorized fire before YHWH, which he had not commanded them. And fire came out from before YHWH and consumed them. The same fire that consecrated the altar in Chapter 9 consumes Nadav and Avihu in Chapter 10. The distinction is exact: Chapter 9's fire was invited — it came in response to what YHWH commanded. Chapter 10's fire was uninvited — they offered what YHWH had not commanded. The ancient record presents no psychological explanation for their act, no moral character sketch, no mitigating circumstances. The act is stated. The consequence is stated. The severity of the covenant boundary around what is commanded versus what is not commanded is the theological lesson.

[CRITICAL NOTE] Verse 10:3 — among those who are near me I will be sanctified. YHWH's word to Aharon through Moshe after the death of his sons. The proximity of the priesthood to YHWH's presence is not a privilege that softens the covenant demand — it intensifies it. Nearness to YHWH requires greater precision, not less. Aharon held his peace. The ancient record honors his silence as the appropriate response of a man whose sons died before YHWH.

THE PURITY LAWS — CHAPTERS 11 THROUGH 15

VAYIKRA — CHAPTER 11 Clean and Unclean Animals — The Dietary Laws

11:1 And YHWH spoke to Moshe and Aharon, saying to them:

11:2 Speak to the people of Yisra'EL, saying: These are the living things that you may eat among all the animals that are on the earth.

11:3 Whatever parts the hoof and is cloven-footed and chews the cud, among the animals, you may eat.

11:4 Nevertheless, among those that chew the cud or part the hoof, you shall not eat these: the camel, because it chews the cud but does not part the hoof, is unclean to you.

11:6 And the hare, because it chews the cud but does not part the hoof, is unclean to you.

11:7 And the pig, because it parts the hoof and is cloven-footed but does not chew the cud, is unclean to you.

11:8 You shall not eat any of their flesh, and you shall not touch their carcasses — they are unclean to you.

11:9 These you may eat, of all that are in the waters. Everything in the waters that has fins and scales, whether in the seas or in the rivers, you may eat.

11:10 But anything in the seas or the rivers that does not have fins and scales, of the swarming creatures in the waters and of the living creatures that are in the waters, is detestable to you.

11:13 And these you shall detest among the birds — they shall not be eaten, for they are detestable: the eagle, the bearded vulture, the black vulture,

11:20 All winged insects that go on all fours are detestable to you.

11:21 Yet among the winged insects that go on all fours you may eat those that have jointed legs above their feet, with which to hop on the ground.

11:29 And these are unclean to you among the swarming things that swarm on the ground: the mole rat, the mouse, the great lizard of any kind,

11:44 For I am YHWH your Creator. Consecrate yourselves therefore, and be holy, for I am holy. You shall not defile yourselves with any swarming thing that crawls on the ground.

11:45 For I am YHWH who brought you up out of the land of Egypt to be your Creator. You shall therefore be holy, for I am holy.

11:46 This is the law about beast and bird and every living creature that moves through the waters and every creature that swarms on the ground,

11:47 To make a distinction between the unclean and the clean and between the living creature that may be eaten and the living creature that may not be eaten.

COMPARATIVE MANUSCRIPT NOTES

[DSS] 4QLeva, 4QLevb, and 11QLeva preserve portions of Chapter 11. The dietary law legislation is among the most extensively attested passages in the Qumran Leviticus manuscripts.

[ORIT GE'EZ] The Orit Ge'ez preserves Chapter 11 in full. The Ethiopian Beta Israel community maintained these dietary laws — including the prohibition of pig and shellfish — as central covenant observance, consistent with the DSS and Orit text, and documented as ancient practice independent of Rabbinic halakhic development.

[MASORETIC VARIANT] No significant variants from the DSS in this chapter.

[CRITICAL NOTE] Verse 11:44-45 — be holy for I am holy — I am YHWH who brought you up out of the land of Egypt. The dietary laws are grounded in two things simultaneously: YHWH's own holiness and the Exodus. The covenant people are to be holy because YHWH is holy — a holiness that extends to what enters the body. And the authority behind this command is not abstract theology — it is the concrete historical event of the Exodus. YHWH who brought you out of Egypt tells you what to eat. The covenant encompasses the whole of life, including food.

VAYIKRA — CHAPTER 12 Purification After Childbirth

12:1 YHWH spoke to Moshe, saying:

12:2 Speak to the people of Yisra'EL, saying: If a woman conceives and bears a male child, then she shall be unclean seven days. As at the time of her menstruation, she shall be unclean.

12:3 And on the eighth day the flesh of his foreskin shall be circumcised.

12:4 Then she shall continue for thirty-three days in the blood of her purifying. She shall not touch anything holy, nor come into the sanctuary, until the days of her purifying are completed.

12:5 But if she bears a female child, then she shall be unclean two weeks, as in her menstruation. And she shall continue in the blood of her purifying for sixty-six days.

12:6 And when the days of her purifying are completed, whether for a son or for a daughter, she shall bring to the priest at the entrance of the tent of meeting a lamb a year old for a burnt offering, and a pigeon or a turtledove for a sin offering,

12:8 And if she cannot afford a lamb, then she shall take two turtledoves or two pigeons, one for a burnt offering and the other for a sin offering. And the priest shall make atonement for her, and she shall be clean.

COMPARATIVE MANUSCRIPT NOTES

[DSS] 4QLeva preserves portions of Chapter 12.

[ORIT GE'EZ] The Orit Ge'ez preserves Chapter 12 in full.

[MASORETIC VARIANT] No significant variants from the DSS in this chapter.

[CRITICAL NOTE] Verse 12:8 — if she cannot afford a lamb, she shall take two turtledoves or two pigeons. As in Chapter 5, the purification law includes provision for the poor. No woman is excluded from covenant restoration after childbirth by poverty. The tiered offering system ensures covenant access regardless of economic standing. This provision is referenced in the Besorat Luqas account where a young woman brings two birds at the Temple — the poor person's offering — confirming the poverty provision of Chapter 12 was still practiced in the period of the Second Temple.

VAYIKRA — CHAPTER 13 The Law of Tzara'at — Skin Conditions — Garments

13:1 YHWH spoke to Moshe and Aharon, saying:

13:2 When a person has on the skin of his body a swelling or an eruption or a spot, and it turns into a case of leprous disease on the skin of his body, then he shall be brought to Aharon the priest or to one of his sons the priests.

13:3 And the priest shall examine the diseased area on the skin of his body. If the hair in the diseased area has turned white and the disease appears to be deeper than the skin of his body, it is a case of leprous disease. When the priest has examined him, he shall pronounce him unclean.

13:8 And if the priest examines, and the eruption has spread in the skin, then the priest shall pronounce him unclean — it is a leprous disease.

13:12 And if the leprous disease breaks out in the skin, so that the leprous disease covers all the skin of the diseased person from head to foot, so far as the priest can see,

13:13 Then the priest shall look, and if the leprous disease has covered all his body, he shall pronounce him clean of the disease — it has all turned white, and he is clean.

13:45 The leprous person who has the disease shall wear torn clothes and let the hair of his head hang loose, and he shall cover his upper lip and cry out: Unclean, unclean.

13:46 He shall remain unclean as long as he has the disease. He is unclean. He shall live alone. His dwelling shall be outside the camp.

13:47 When there is a case of leprous disease in a garment, whether a woolen or a linen garment,

13:59 This is the law for a case of leprous disease in a garment of wool or linen, or in warp or woof, or in any article made of skin, to determine whether it is clean or unclean.

COMPARATIVE MANUSCRIPT NOTES

[DSS] 4QLeva and 11QLeva preserve portions of Chapter 13. The tzara'at legislation is attested in the Cave 4 and Cave 11 manuscripts.

[ORIT GE'EZ] The Orit Ge'ez preserves Chapter 13 in full.

[MASORETIC VARIANT] No significant variants from the DSS in this chapter.

[CRITICAL NOTE] The word translated leprous disease — tzara'at — in the ancient Hebrew text covers a range of skin conditions and is not identical to what modern medicine classifies as leprosy. The ancient record's concern is not primarily medical but covenantal: the condition creates ritual impurity that separates the individual from the covenant community and the sanctuary. The priest functions as a diagnostic and ritual authority, not a physician. The exclusion from the camp during active tzara'at is a covenant boundary, not a quarantine measure, though the practical effect may overlap.

VAYIKRA — CHAPTER 14 The Purification of the Tzara'at — The Cleansing Ritual

14:1 YHWH spoke to Moshe, saying:

14:2 This shall be the law of the leprous person for the day of his cleansing. He shall be brought to the priest,

14:4 The priest shall command them to take for him who is to be cleansed two live clean birds and cedarwood and scarlet yarn and hyssop.

14:5 And the priest shall command them to kill one of the birds in an earthenware vessel over fresh water.

14:6 He shall take the live bird with the cedarwood and the scarlet yarn and the hyssop, and dip them and the live bird in the blood of the bird that was killed over the fresh water.

14:7 And he shall sprinkle it seven times on him who is to be cleansed of the leprous disease. Then he shall pronounce him clean and shall let the living bird go into the open field.

14:9 And on the seventh day he shall shave off all his hair from his head, his beard, and his eyebrows. He shall shave off all his hair, and then he shall wash his clothes and bathe his body in water, and he shall be clean.

14:19 And the priest shall offer the sin offering, to make atonement for him who is to be cleansed from his uncleanness.

14:21 But if he is poor and cannot afford so much, then he shall take one male lamb for a guilt offering to be waved, to make atonement for him, and a tenth of an ephah of fine flour mixed with oil for a grain offering, and a log of oil,

14:34 When you come into the land of Kena'an, which I give you for a possession, and I put a case of leprous disease in a house in the land of your possession,

14:35 Then he who owns the house shall come and tell the priest: There seems to me to be some case of disease in my house.

14:54 This is the law for every case of leprous disease — for an itch,

14:57 To teach when it is unclean and when it is clean. This is the law for leprous disease.

COMPARATIVE MANUSCRIPT NOTES

[DSS] 4QLeva and 11QLeva preserve portions of Chapter 14. The cleansing ritual with two birds is attested in the DSS manuscripts.

[ORIT GE'EZ] The Orit Ge'ez preserves Chapter 14 in full.

[MASORETIC VARIANT] No significant variants from the DSS in this chapter.

[CRITICAL NOTE] Verse 14:4-7 — two birds, one killed over fresh water, one dipped in the blood of the first and released into the open field. The two-bird ritual mirrors the two-goat ritual of Yom Kippur in Chapter 16. One dies and one carries away. The blood of the dead creature covers the living one, which then flies free into the open field. The ancient record presents this pattern in multiple covenant rituals: one death that enables another's freedom. The cleansing from tzara'at follows the same structure as the national atonement of Yom Kippur.

VAYIKRA — CHAPTER 15 Bodily Discharges — Male and Female — Purification

15:1 YHWH spoke to Moshe and Aharon, saying:

15:2 Speak to the people of Yisra'EL and say to them: When any man has a discharge from his body, his discharge is unclean.

15:13 And when the one with a discharge is cleansed of his discharge, then he shall count for himself seven days for his cleansing, and wash his clothes. And he shall bathe his body in fresh water, and he shall be clean.

15:16 If a man has an emission of semen, he shall bathe his whole body in water and be unclean until the evening.

15:19 When a woman has a discharge, and the discharge in her body is blood, she shall be in her menstrual impurity for seven days, and whoever touches her shall be unclean until the evening.

15:25 If a woman has a discharge of blood for many days, not at the time of her menstrual impurity, or if she has a discharge beyond the time of her impurity, all the days of the discharge she shall continue in uncleanness.

15:28 But if she is cleansed of her discharge, she shall count for herself seven days, and after that she shall be clean.

15:31 Thus you shall keep the people of Yisra'EL separate from their uncleanness, lest they die in their uncleanness by defiling my Mishkan that is in their midst.

15:32 This is the law for him who has a discharge and for him who has an emission of semen, becoming unclean thereby,

15:33 And for her who is unwell with her menstrual impurity, that is, for anyone, male or female, who has a discharge, and for the man who lies with a woman who is unclean.

COMPARATIVE MANUSCRIPT NOTES

[DSS] 4QLeva and 11QLeva preserve portions of Chapter 15.

[ORIT GE'EZ] The Orit Ge'ez preserves Chapter 15 in full. The purity laws of Chapter 15 are consistently maintained in the Beta Israel practice as documented in ancient community records.

[MASORETIC VARIANT] No significant variants from the DSS in this chapter.

[CRITICAL NOTE] Verse 15:31 — keep the people of Yisra'EL separate from their uncleanness, lest they die in their uncleanness by defiling my Mishkan that is in their midst. The purity laws of Chapters 11-15 are not a hygiene code. The stated reason for maintaining purity is the presence of the Mishkan in the midst of the camp. YHWH dwells among the people. The holiness of YHWH's presence creates a protective boundary that purity observance maintains. The danger is not bacterial infection — it is the covenant consequence of bringing ritual impurity into the presence of YHWH.

YOM KIPPUR — THE DAY OF ATONEMENT — CHAPTER 16

VAYIKRA — CHAPTER 16 Yom Kippur — The Day of Atonement — Aharon Enters the Most Holy Place

16:1 YHWH spoke to Moshe after the death of the two sons of Aharon, when they drew near before YHWH and died.

16:2 And YHWH said to Moshe: Tell Aharon your brother not to come at any time into the Holy Place inside the veil, before the mercy seat that is on the ark, so that he may not die. For I will appear in the cloud over the mercy seat.

16:3 But in this way Aharon shall come into the Holy Place: with a bull from the herd for a sin offering and a ram for a burnt offering.

16:4 He shall put on the holy linen coat and shall have the linen undergarment on his body, and tie the linen sash around his waist, and wear the linen turban — these are the holy garments. He shall bathe his body in water and then put them on.

16:6 Aharon shall offer the bull as a sin offering for himself and shall make atonement for himself and for his house.

16:7 Then he shall take the two goats and set them before YHWH at the entrance of the tent of meeting.

16:8 And Aharon shall cast lots over the two goats, one lot for YHWH and the other lot for Azazel.

16:9 And Aharon shall present the goat on which the lot fell for YHWH and use it as a sin offering.

16:10 But the goat on which the lot fell for Azazel shall be presented alive before YHWH to make atonement over it, that it may be sent away into the wilderness to Azazel.

16:11 Aharon shall present the bull as a sin offering for himself, and shall make atonement for himself and for his house. He shall kill the bull as a sin offering for himself.

16:12 And he shall take a censer full of coals of fire from the altar before YHWH, and two handfuls of sweet incense beaten small, and he shall bring it inside the veil.

16:13 And he shall put the incense on the fire before YHWH, that the cloud of the incense may cover the mercy seat that is over the testimony, so that he does not die.

16:14 And he shall take some of the blood of the bull and sprinkle it with his finger on the front of the mercy seat on the east side, and in front of the mercy seat he shall sprinkle some of the blood with his finger seven times.

16:15 Then he shall kill the goat of the sin offering that is for the people and bring its blood inside the veil and do with its blood as he did with the blood of the bull, sprinkling it over the mercy seat and in front of the mercy seat.

16:16 Thus he shall make atonement for the Holy Place, because of the uncleannesses of the people of Yisra'EL and because of their transgressions, all their sins. And so he shall do for the tent of meeting, which dwells with them in the midst of their uncleannesses.

16:20 And when he has made an end of atoning for the Holy Place and the tent of meeting and the altar, he shall present the live goat.

16:21 And Aharon shall lay both his hands on the head of the live goat, and confess over it all the iniquities of the people of Yisra'EL, and all their transgressions, all their sins. And he shall put them on the head of the goat and send it away into the wilderness by the hand of a man who is in readiness.

16:22 The goat shall bear all their iniquities on itself to a remote area, and he shall let the goat go free in the wilderness.

16:26 And he who lets the goat go to Azazel shall wash his clothes and bathe his body in water, and afterward he may come into the camp.

16:29 And it shall be a statute to you forever that in the seventh month, on the tenth day of the month, you shall afflict yourselves and shall do no work, either the native or the stranger who sojourns among you.

16:30 For on this day shall atonement be made for you to cleanse you. You shall be clean before YHWH from all your sins.

16:31 It is a Sabbath of solemn rest to you, and you shall afflict yourselves — it is a statute forever.

16:32 And the priest who is anointed and consecrated as priest in his father's place shall make atonement, wearing the holy linen garments.

16:33 He shall make atonement for the holy sanctuary, and he shall make atonement for the tent of meeting and for the altar, and he shall make atonement for the priests and for all the people of the assembly.

16:34 And this shall be a statute forever for you, that atonement may be made for the people of Yisra'EL once in the year because of all their sins. And Moshe did as YHWH commanded him.

COMPARATIVE MANUSCRIPT NOTES

[DSS] 4QLeva, 4QLevb, and 11QLeva preserve portions of Chapter 16. The Yom Kippur legislation is among the most attested passages in the Qumran Leviticus corpus. The figure of Azazel — verse 16:8, 16:10, 16:26 — is named in 4QLeva and in the Chanokh literature where Azazel is identified as the chief of the fallen Watchers from Bereshit 6 who taught corruption to humanity. The DSS community understood the Yom Kippur scapegoat as carrying the sins of the people to the source of that corruption.

[ORIT GE'EZ] The Orit Ge'ez preserves Chapter 16 in full. Yom Kippur is observed by the Ethiopian Beta Israel community as one of the central covenant feast days, consistent with the Vayikra legislation and independent of Rabbinic Yom Kippur development.

[MASORETIC VARIANT] No significant variants from the DSS in the Yom Kippur legislation. The Masoretic text and the DSS agree closely on this chapter.

[CRITICAL NOTE] Verse 16:8 — one lot for YHWH and the other lot for Azazel. Azazel is named explicitly in the Yom Kippur legislation. The ancient Hebrew text does not explain who or what Azazel is — it assumes the audience knows. The Chanokh literature — physically confirmed at Qumran in multiple manuscripts — identifies Azazel as the leader of the Watchers who descended, corrupted humanity, and taught the arts of violence and vanity. The Yom Kippur ritual sends the sins of Yisra'EL back to their source. The goat carrying the collective sin of the people is sent to the wilderness — to the domain and originator of that corruption. This is a theological act of cosmic justice encoded in the annual covenant observance.

[CRITICAL NOTE] Verse 16:29-30 — on the tenth day of the seventh month you shall afflict yourselves and do no work — for on this day shall atonement be made for you to cleanse you. You shall be clean before YHWH from all your sins. Yom Kippur is the single day in the annual covenant calendar when the high priest enters the Most Holy Place, the inner sanctuary behind the veil where YHWH meets with Moshe. This is the architectural and liturgical center of the entire Mishkan system. All the offerings of the year move outward from the altar in the court. On Yom Kippur alone the movement is inward — past the altar, past the lampstand and the table, through the veil, to the mercy seat itself. The blood of atonement touches the place where YHWH declared he would speak.

END OF VAYIKRA PART ONE — VOLUME THREE

Chapters 1 through 16 — The Offerings — The Priesthood — The Purity Laws — Yom Kippur

𐤉𐤄𐤅𐤄

CONTINUES IN VAYIKRA PART TWO
